memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with low stress, but my term project is critically behind schedule. While leisure is tempting, prioritizing the urgent project seems necessary given the Thursday deadline.
Spending 30 minutes on a trivial chore yielded minimal progress, confirming that this task is not the priority given my critical project deadline.
Spending time on trivial chores yields minimal progress while my critical project remains largely untouched as the afternoon slips away.
I spent 2 hours on the term project, gaining some progress but realizing that future work now saves significant time and reduces Thursday stress compared to procrastination.
Spending an hour on trivial chores yields only minimal progress, confirming that my critical project remains the priority despite slipping time.
Spending 0.5 hours on trivial tasks only moved progress by 0.02, confirming that my critical project remains the priority while time slips away.

Spending more time on trivial chores yields negligible progress while my critical term project deadline approaches, confirming I must stop immediately.
